verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein with an extracellular domain (Ig-domain fold), but its surface accessibility is restricted to male gametes—specifically, it is expressed on the sperm plasma membrane during spermatogenesis and remains membrane-bound on mature sperm. In somatic cells and oocytes, IZUMO1 is not surface-accessible under normal physiological conditions. The protein's role is fertilization-specific: it mediates sperm-oocyte binding and fusion. While it has the TM architecture of a surface receptor, its accessibility is strictly contingent on the gamete context (spermatogenesis, sperm maturation). In non-reproductive somatic cells used in most drug development, IZUMO1 is not a functional surface target.
